evaluation.summary: Empirical gap variances for primorial bases grow faster than (log P_k)^{1.168}, with a fitted exponent of -2.67 that contradicts the conjectured scaling.
next_directions: Extend the computation to much larger primorials to obtain a reliable variance estimate.
Develop a rigorous statistical test to assess whether the observed deviation from 1.168 is significant.
Derive or test a theoretical model linking gap variance to the distribution of prime gaps in primorial bases.
